rule_text: Shallow draught vessels may anchor in the fork between Williamstown and Port Melbourne Channels. Depth 6.5 to 7.5 metres.
Trigger safety protocol for ultra-large container vessels
Documents required to be produced to Australian Border Force at first port are: Form 5 - Part 4 relating to the vessel’s stores. Forms 3a and 3b - Crew report. Form 13 – Pre arrival report.
Initiate storm watch procedures during rapid atmospheric pressure decline.
Activate freeze protection systems when water temperature approaches freezing point.
Restrict vessel maneuvering during long swell periods to ensure mooring security.
Vessel arrival delay exceeding 6 hours requires berth schedule reassignment and notification to terminal operator.
Flag vessels exceeding maximum terminal length limits for manual review
Immigration clearance procedures for crew members of non-military ships require all foreign crew to hold a valid passport with a marine crew visa.
Restrict access for vessels below minimum economic size for deep-sea terminals
Before a vessel enters port waters the owner of the vessel must, unless VRCA otherwise agrees in writing, appoint an agent for the vessel, who may be the Master.
Inbound vessels should establish contact with Lonsdale VTS and, on clearing the South Channel, make contact with Geelong Port Marine Control.
The Master of a vessel of 200 gross tons or more must cause the vessel to fly its national colours between the hours of sunrise and sunset while within port waters.
At the time of placing a pilot order for departure, Geelong Port Marine Control is to be advised if the vessel is slow-steaming as a consequence of repairs undertaken during the port stay.
Tugs must be ordered as per the Minimum Towage Table.
